cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this problem step by step. So the question is whether following a set of movement instructions will bring someone back to their starting point. The instructions are: Always face forward. Take 3 steps right. Take 1 step backward. Take 6 steps left. Take 1 step forward. Take 3 steps right. The options are Yes or No. First, I need to visualize the movements. The key here is to track the direction changes and position changes correctly. Let me break down each step. Starting position: Let's assume the starting point is (0,0) on a coordinate plane. Facing forward is the default direction. For simplicity, I'll consider the forward direction as positive y-axis (up) and right as positive x-axis (right). So when moving right, it's along the x-axis; moving forward is along the y-axis. 1. Take 3 steps right. So moving from (0,0) to (3,0). Because each step right increases the x-coordinate by 1. 2. Take 1 step backward. Since facing forward is along positive y-axis, backward is negative y-axis. So moving from (3,0) to (3, -1). Wait, wait. Wait, but the direction hasn't changed.
